channel.title: ClimateEmergency on Nature Works
channel.description: Recent content in ClimateEmergency on Nature Works

item.title: A real intersectional garden
item.description:
A forest garden is an intersectional garden, not in a limited sense of intersecting, but because it’s a response truly intersectional forces

I was listening to the latest episode of my favourite gardening podcast Roots and All, and it was an interview with Dr Anne Karpf about her book How Women Can Save The Planet. http://localhost:1313/blog/230322-real-intersectional-garden/

item.title: Dream-like cinema
item.description:
Cinema, like dreams, can help work through our lives

I watched World War Z with my teenaged son the other night. http://localhost:1313/blog/230306-dreams/

item.title: Vision Pt. 1
item.description:
“So, what does this Nature & Climate Emergency garden actually look like?”

I have always thought that forest gardens are the ideal Climate Emergency garden, ‘tough on Climate Change, tough on the causes of Climate Change’ as Tony Blair (remember him? http://localhost:1313/blog/230227-vision/

item.title: Where does the water go?
item.description:
In a Climate Emergency, figure out how to hold on to your precious water.

The rain here in West Wales has been minimal in February, normally an incredibly wet month. http://localhost:1313/blog/230219-water/

item.title: 1.5°C within 10-15 years
item.description:
This is remarkably bad news that hasn’t really hit the headlines

“@PNASNews suggests earth will cross the 1.5 degrees C temperature increase threshold in the next decade (within just 10 to 15 years) and is likely to exceed 2 degrees C by around 2060. http://localhost:1313/blog/230203-onepointfive/

item.title: This is why I’m a climate activist
item.description:
There will be no bumblebees in the UK by the end of the century if we hit 3.5°C

I went to an online “Evening for Wildlife Gardeners” tonight, organised by the Wildlife Garden Forum, and it was really very good and really very sad. http://localhost:1313/blog/230112-life/

item.title: Warming Stripes
item.description: There is a #ClimateEmergency and the rate of change is incredibly fast. http://localhost:1313/blog/warming-stripes/

item.title: Are you ready?
item.description:
The climate and ecological emergency is happening. A forest garden is fundamentally sustainable, reducing carbon emissions & increasing biodiversity, whilst providing a resilient food system in case of emergency.

The Covid-19 coronavirus crisis shines a light on how ill-prepared and unequal our society has become. http://localhost:1313/blog/are-you-ready/

item.title: Peat Free Nurseries
item.description:
Does your nursery use peat-free compost? A list of nurseries that do, compiled by Nic Wilson 🙏

Using peat-based compost contributes to #ClimateBreakdown and wildlife habitat loss. Best thing you can do is use a peat-free compost (such as Sylvagrow, FertileFibre, Dalefoot etc) and buy your plants from a nursery that use peat-free compost. http://localhost:1313/blog/peat-free-nurseries/

item.title: PeatFree Climate Emergency
item.description:
Using peat-based compost contributes to the destruction of wildlife habitats and the release of vast amounts of greenhouse gases. Profit, convenience & ignorance; it is the story of Climate Breakdown.

The peat extraction industry is a microcosm of the wider Climate Emergency. http://localhost:1313/blog/peatfree-climate-emergency/

item.title: DBIF
item.description:
An invaluable tool in the wildlife gardeners’ toolbox, The Database of Insects and their Food Plants details scientifically corroborated interactions between insects and plants

The Database of Insects and their Food Plants (DBIF for short) is a fantastic resource for wildlife gardeners. http://localhost:1313/dbif/

item.title: Emergency
item.description:
A forest garden works with nature to grow edible crops, which is why it’s good for combatting Climate Emergency and Mass Extinction. Here are 11 reasons why.

The Climate Emergency and Mass Extinction is caused by living in an unsustainable way. http://localhost:1313/emergency/
